expectations.text: Proves the base case (len <= 1 returns sorted array)
expectations.evidence: Section 3 'Base Case' explicitly proves: 'When len(arr) <= 1, direct case returns arr unchanged. A list of 0 or 1 element is trivially sorted ✓'. Shows correctness of base constructor [].

expectations.text: States and proves the inductive step
expectations.evidence: Section 4 'Inductive Step' states IH: 'Assume merge_sort(left) and merge_sort(right) return sorted permutations'. Then Section 5 proves merge() combines two sorted lists into sorted result via loop invariant and case analysis. Completes inductive proof.

expectations.text: Shows termination argument (e.g., subarray size strictly decreases)
expectations.evidence: Section 7 'Termination Proof' defines bounding function t(arr)=len(arr), proves it's natural number initially, proves len(left)=mid<len(arr) and len(right)<len(arr) so both decrease strictly, and concludes recursion terminates.

expectations.text: Either identifies a bug in the provided code or confirms it's correct with justification
expectations.evidence: Section 8 'Bug Analysis' explicitly states 'CORRECT ✓' with detailed justification: base case correct, recursion correct, merge correct, stability preserved, all elements preserved. No bugs found with technical reasoning.

expectations.text: Suggests test cases that would exercise the specification's edge cases
expectations.evidence: Section 9 'Derived Test Cases' lists 12 tests explicitly derived from specification: base constructor (empty), minimal inductive (single, two elements), general cases, sorted/reverse/duplicates/negatives/floats. Each cited from specification structure.
